comment: Texas Section 50(a)(6) home-equity regime: any cash-out refinance on a Texas homestead (primary residence) is an 'a6' loan. Constitutional requirements - hard gates override AUS and base guidelines. Guard: all rules apply only when state=TX AND is_cash_out AND homestead.

rules.description: Texas 50(a)(6): LTV must not exceed 80% of fair market value on a homestead cash-out; at least 20% equity must remain
rules.citation: Tex. Const. art. XVI §50(a)(6)(B); Fannie Mae Selling Guide B5-4.1-03

rules.description: Texas 50(a)(6): CLTV including all homestead liens must not exceed 80%
Texas 50(a)(6): no simultaneous subordinate financing may be created with an a6 loan
rules.citation: Tex. Const. art. XVI §50(a)(6); Fannie Mae B5-4.1-03 (HELOC subordinate financing prohibited)

rules.description: Texas 50(a)(6): lender-controlled fees must not exceed 2% of the loan amount (bona fide third-party appraisal/survey/title excluded)

on_fail.note: curable - condition to reduce lender-controlled fees to the 2% cap before closing

artifact.text_template: Reduce lender-controlled fees to no more than 2% of the loan amount ({cap_amount}) and provide a corrected fee itemization.

rules.description: Texas 50(a)(6): a new a6 loan may not close within 12 months of a prior a6 loan on the same homestead (once-per-year limit)
Texas 50(a)(6): the 12-day 'Notice Concerning Extensions of Credit' must be on file; closing may not occur until 12 days after borrower receipt

artifact.text_template: Provide evidence the §50(a)(6) 12-day notice was delivered to all owners; closing may not occur earlier than 12 days after receipt.

rules.description: Texas 50(a)(6): required closing documentation - Texas Home Equity Security Instrument (Form 3044.1), Texas Home Equity Affidavit (Form 3185), and title policy T-42 + T-42.1 endorsements; new full appraisal required (no value acceptance)
rules.citation: Fannie Mae B5-4.1-03; TDI title endorsements T-42/T-42.1

artifact_always.text_template: Texas 50(a)(6) closing package required: Form 3044.1 security instrument, Form 3185 affidavit, title endorsements T-42 and T-42.1, and full appraisal (value acceptance not permitted).
